title: acer glabrum var. diffusum
intro: Acer glabrum var. diffusum, also known as Rocky Mountain Maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which contain tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The medicinal properties of Acer glabrum var. diffusum have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems, and have also been used to reduce pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
